_meta.note: A+J=T (Tyrion Targaryen) cluster: mint a-plus-j-equals-t (concept.theory). Source: ASX video eqVhKOxmJCw (Tyrion Targaryen: is Tyrion the Mad King's son?). All quotes byte-verified against sources/chapters/*.md at cited lines. Barristan's 'wanted'/kitchen-gossip framing (beats ajt-B01/B04) held out as premise prose per the no-edges-from-self-hedged-rumors rule; only the unhedged wedding-night 'liberties' clause from the same passage is edged (A1). S216 adjudication: A2 DROPPED to premise prose (Tywin's cruelty fits every reading — consistent-with, not evidence-for; T8/B8 precedent); A1 context note appended.

edges.quote: Tywin Lannister was not a man to forget such words, or the … the liberties your father took during the bedding.
edges.note: Barristan's account to Dany of Aerys's wedding-night behavior toward Joanna Lannister at her wedding to Tywin — the theory's central circumstantial basis. Secondhand court knowledge from a Kingsguard; tier-4 because the same passage opens with Barristan calling the broader 'wanted' framing 'kitchen gossip' (held out as prose, not edged), and this specific clause is not itself re-hedged. S216 fresh-verify adjudication: kept tier-4 with context acknowledged — the surrounding passage is hedge-saturated (Barristan's own 'kitchen gossip' framing, immediate regret), but the liberties clause itself is Barristan's direct careful assertion, not the gossip layer.

edges.quote: You . . . you are no . . . no son of mine.
edges.note: Tywin's dying words to Tyrion, read plainly, deny paternity. Tier-4 (not tier-3) because the context is at least as consistent with a dying insult as a literal genealogical claim — Tyrion's own reply ('I believe I'm you writ small') reasserts resemblance rather than denying kinship.

edges.quote: but Tyrion is Tywin’s son, not you.
edges.note: Genna's plain, unhedged statement to Jaime that Tyrion is Tywin's biological son. Stronger and less caveated than the SUPPORTS edges, hence tier-3. The video reframes this as Genna recognizing Tyrion's Tywin-like temperament rather than literal proof of paternity, but the line itself is a flat paternity claim from someone with no evident reason to lie.

edges.quote: That was how Lord Tywin Lannister dealt with whores.
edges.note: Cersei's memory of young Tywin publicly humiliating his own father Tytos's mistress, mirrored decades later when Tyrion finds Shae wearing Tywin's Hand-of-the-King jewels in Tywin's bed. Supplies a generational, parentage-free explanation for Tywin's severity toward women and the men associated with them, undercutting the theory's need for a secret-Targaryen-parentage motive. Indirect/alternate-explanation edge, hence tier-4.
